channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: New bill would mandate gyms to carry equipment for Americans with disabilities
item.description: Congressional Democrats are pushing for a massive overhaul to regulatory policy concerning Americans with disabilities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Americans are exhausted by political news. TV ratings and a new AP-NORC poll show they're tuning out
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As a Democrat who immersed himself in political news during the presidential campaign, Ziad Aunallah has much in common with many Americans since the election. He's tuned out. “People are mentally exhausted,” said Aunallah, 45, of San Diego.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump has pressed for voting changes. GOP majorities in Congress will try to make that happen
item.description: Republicans in Congress plan to move quickly in their effort to overhaul the nation’s voting procedures, seeing an opportunity with control of the White House and both chambers of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Christina A. Cassidy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump announces ambassador to Panama amid fight over canal
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday announced Miami-Dade County Commissioner Kevin Marino Cabrera as his pick for U.S. ambassador to Panama. The announcement comes as Trump is sparking a feud with the U.S. ally over control of the Panama Canal.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Fate of Trump's Cabinet picks unclear as Republicans prepare to take power in Senate
item.description: The fate of President-elect Donald Trump’s Cabinet is still unclear after Republican senators spent much of December carefully dodging questions.
item.dc:creator: Mary Clare Jalonick, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump vows to pursue executions after Biden commutes most of federal death row
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump says he'll “vigorously pursue” capital punishment after President Joe Biden commuted the sentences of most people on federal death row partly to stop Trump from pushing forward their executions.
item.dc:creator: Adriana Gomez Licon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump vows to direct DOJ to pursue death penalty in wake of Biden clemencies
item.description: President-elect Trump vowed Tuesday to direct his Department of Justice (DOJ) to pursue the death penalty in the wake of President Biden’s clemencies of nearly every federal death row inmate.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden signs defense bill despite objections to ban on transgender health care for military children
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Monday signed into law a defense bill that authorizes significant pay raises for junior enlisted service members, aims to counter China's growing power and boosts overall military spending to $895 billion despite his objections to language stripping coverage
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Inside the Gaetz ethics report, a trove of new details alleging payments for sex and drug use
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House Ethics Committee's long-awaited report on Matt Gaetz documents a trove of salacious allegations, including sex with an underage girl, that tanked the Florida Republican's bid to lead the Justice Department.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ethics releases Matt Gaetz report: 5 takeaways
item.description: The report — which spans 42 pages and includes several more in exhibits — found “substantial evidence” that Gaetz violated House rules and state and federal laws.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Bill Clinton hospitalized with fever but in good spirits, spokesperson says
item.description: Former President Bill Clinton was admitted Monday to Georgetown University Medical Center in Washington after developing a fever.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A history of the Panama Canal
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Teddy Roosevelt once declared the Panama Canal “one of the feats to which the people of this republic will look back with the highest pride.” More than a century later, Donald Trump is threatening to take back the waterway for the same republic.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, JUAN ZAMORANO and GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An ex-Washington police officer is convicted of lying about leaks to the Proud Boys leader
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A retired police officer in the nation's capital was convicted Monday of lying to authorities about leaking confidential information to the leader of the Proud Boys extremist group. U.S. District Judge Amy Berman Jackson convicted former Metropolitan Police Department Lt.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden didn't remove these 3 inmates from death row
item.description: Those who received the Monday commutations of their sentences saw them go from execution to life with no possibility of parole. However, three “hard cases” were not among the commutations.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Biden gives life in prison to 37 of 40 federal death row inmates
item.description: The move spares the lives of people convicted in killings, including the slayings of police and military officers, people on federal land and those involved in deadly bank robberies or drug deals, as well as the killings of guards or prisoners in federal facilities.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Gaetz sues Ethics Committee in last-minute bid to block release of report
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) filed a lawsuit Monday against the House Ethics Committee in a last-minute attempt to block the panel from releasing its report on him.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Many Americans have come to rely on Chinese-made drones. Now lawmakers want to ban them
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Russell Hedrick, a North Carolina farmer, flies drones to spray fertilizers on his corn, soybean and wheat fields at a fraction of what it would cost him to use a conventional ground spreader.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden commutes sentences of 37 death row inmates to life without parole
item.description: "In good conscience, I cannot stand back and let a new administration resume executions that I halted," Biden said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: White House made 'friendliest threat' over Biden's decline: DNC committee member
item.description: According to Lindy Li, the official told her, "You're one of the President's most loyal supporters. We know you're going to stay loyal.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Trump pushes back on 'President Musk' claims
item.description: “No, he’s not gonna be president, that I can tell you,” Trump said. “And I’m safe, you know why? He can’t be, he wasn’t born in this country.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Trump suggests Panama Canal be returned to US control
item.description: President-elect Trump on Sunday again took on the matter of the Panama Canal, suggesting to a conference of his supporters that it be returned to U.S. control in a remarkable suggestion to push a sovereign country to cede territory.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Incoming Trump press secretary says Day 1 executive order may tackle Title 42
item.description: President-elect Trump’s incoming press secretary Karoline Leavitt said Trump’s Day 1 executive orders may tackle Title 42. Leavitt joined Fox News’s “Sunday Morning Futures,” where she outlined what can be expected from Trump’s first day in office next month.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NJ rep. says it's alarming Feds can't bring down one drone
item.description: Chris Smith is disappointed by what he sees as a lack of transparency by the Biden administration over the New Jersey drone flap.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Government handling of drones a 'catastrophe': Elizondo
item.description: The FAA temporarily banned drone flights in areas of New Jersey and New York, where critical infrastructure is located.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Lara Trump says she will not seek to replace Rubio in Senate
item.description: Lara Trump stepped down as RNC co-chair to “heavily consider” taking on the role in Congress's upper chamber.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Here are the 34 Republicans that voted against the funding bill to avoid shutdown
item.description: More than 30 House Republicans voted against the government funding bill to avert a shutdown on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: What Mayor Adams told Luigi Mangione when they met in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams said he “wanted to send a strong message” when he met accused UnitedHealthcare CEO killer Luigi Mangione in New York City on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Nevada election leaders find nearly 200 cases of double votes from November
item.description: The Nevada Secretary of State’s Office is investigating 180 double votes from the 2024 general election, a report released Friday indicated.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News
